description: Work queue for proof DAG extraction. Agents pick from here.

queue.reason: Constrains Tao's method — proves regularity in intermediate regime. 40 mathEnvs, in index.
Prerequisite for Tao lem:1.3 — 3 cross-paper edges depend on this. 107 mathEnvs, in index.
Direct ancestor — Waleffe dyadic models. 2 cross-paper edges. 4 mathEnvs, in index.
Tao's earlier hyperdissipative regularity. 3 mathEnvs (1 theorem, 2 remarks). Monolithic proof.
Hou-Wang quasi-exact 1D model — lateral comparison to Tao. 22 mathEnvs, in index.
Seregin — rules out blowup scenarios. 10 mathEnvs, in index.
Miao-Nie-Ye — non-uniqueness in 2D. Parallel development. 22 mathEnvs, in index.
Cheskidov-Dai-Palasek — Type I blow-up + non-uniqueness for any smooth data. Different mechanism from CP. Cited by 2602.19074v1.
Miao-Nie-Ye — non-uniqueness in C_t L^2(R^3). Predecessor of 2602.19074v1, iterative scheme reused. Needs /parse-paper first.
Miao-Nie-Ye — sharp non-uniqueness in L^p_t L^q(R^3). Predecessor of 2602.19074v1.
Seregin — Type II blowups of axisymmetric NSE. Contains proofs of Props 4.1 and 4.2 from 2507.08733v2.
Coiculescu-Palasek — Non-uniqueness from critical data. Closely related technique to 2511.09556 (Prop 3.8, Lem 3.3 cited).
Palasek — Leray-Hopf non-uniqueness for dyadic NSE model. Same author family as 2511.09556.
Hou — BMO^{-1} regularity. Directly used in proof of 2511.09556 Prop 6.2 (right continuity at T_*).
Katz-Pavlovic 2005 Trans. AMS — pre-arXiv, not available on arxiv.org.
Montgomery-Smith — no arXiv version found. Journal-only or misattribution.
Buckmaster-Vicol — non-uniqueness on T^3 (this paper extends to R^3). Primary structural ancestor. Same convex integration scheme.
Buckmaster-Masmoudi-Novack-Vicol — non-conservative Euler weak solutions. Source of inverse divergence iteration step (lem:A.5 in 2412.10404).

Yang et al — FIRST COMPUTER-ASSISTED PROOF of Leray-Hopf nonuniqueness (unforced NSE). The holy grail result. Uses self-similar setting + unstable eigenpair to produce infinitely many Leray-Hopf solutions. Sep 2025.
Palasek — Arbitrary norm growth in BMO^{-1} bounded data. First unbounded growth in well-posed setting. Inverse cascade via repeated squaring. Rules out a priori estimates. Sep 2025.
Buckmaster-Colombo-Vicol — Sharp nonuniqueness in L^p_t L^∞ (Ladyzhenskaya-Prodi-Serrin threshold). Published in Inventiones. Foundational for convex integration approach.
Isett — Proof of Onsager's conjecture. C_t C_x^α non-conservative Euler for α < 1/3. Introduced gluing approximation + Mikado flows. Foundational ancestor of all convex integration NSE work.
REMOVED: Wrong arXiv ID — is nuclear physics paper. Correct paper is 2112.03116 (Albritton-Brué-Colombo), already complete at priority 27.
queue.blocked_reason: WRONG ID: arxiv 2112.09717 is a PREX-II nuclear physics paper, not Cheskidov-Luo NSE. No Cheskidov-Luo "forced NSE Leray" paper identified — possible the Albritton-Brue-Colombo 2112.03116 was meant (priority 27). Needs ID correction.

queue.reason: Wang et al (Hou+DeepMind) — Discovery of Unstable Singularities. Closely related computer-assisted instability approach to 2509.25116. Same Hou group.
queue.blocked_reason: Not indexed in ScienceStack as of 2026-03-11. Needs /parse-paper first.

queue.reason: Luo — Ill-posedness in supercritical Sobolev spaces. Adjacent ill-posedness technique, cited by 2509.18595. Complements norm-growth vs ill-posedness comparison.
queue.blocked_reason: Not indexed in ScienceStack as of 2026-03-11. Needs /parse-paper first.

queue.reason: Luo — Sharp norm inflation for 3D NSE in supercritical spaces (2025). More recent, closely related to 2509.18595; cited there.
Albritton-Brue-Colombo — Non-uniqueness of Leray solutions of forced NSE. First Leray-Hopf non-uniqueness result. Closely related to 2009.06596 and 2112.09717. Cited by CL.
BCV Wild solutions — Hausdorff dim of singular set < 1. Parallel work to 2009.06596; both construct non-Leray solutions with fractal singular sets. Cited by CL.
